old_claim.note: Codified Law provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The corrected scope is legal chattel slavery: the Code of Hammurabi source records slave ownership, sale, fugitive-return, and status rules in a written law code.

source_shape.source_locator: The translation includes legal rules for buying male or female slaves, fugitive slaves, and returning runaway slaves to owners.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source directly records the legal rules that define the corrected legal chattel-slavery scope.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the node is now explicitly about law-enforced property status, the written law-code prerequisite carries the core mechanism rather than broad background context.

ontology_before: Codified Law was a generic enabling background edge for an unsourced Classical chattel-slavery node.
ontology_after: Codified Law is required for the corrected legal-property scope anchored to Hammurabi-era written slave rules.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: slavery_chattel->codified_law is now a required edge.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped away from legal chattel status into all forms of captivity or coerced labor.
The cited Hammurabi source no longer documented slave sale, fugitive return, or slave-status law.
A separate pre-legal slavery node were added and this id were intentionally reassigned to that broader institution.
short_reason: The corrected scope is a law-enforced property status, so codified law is the core mechanism.
